name: Prof. Jane Smith
bio: Jane is a Professor of Computational Psychiatry and the founder of the Mood & Money Lab. Her research combines machine learning, natural language processing, and behavioural science to understand and improve mental health outcomes. She holds a DPhil from Oxford and has been recognised by MIT Technology Review as one of 35 Innovators Under 35.

name: Dr. John Doe
bio: John is a postdoctoral researcher investigating the intersection of financial behaviour and psychological wellbeing. He completed his PhD at the University of Edinburgh and brings expertise in causal inference and econometric modelling.

name: Alice Johnson
bio: Alice is a third-year PhD student studying how passive smartphone sensing can be used for real-time mood monitoring. Before joining the lab she worked as a clinical psychologist, and her research is deeply informed by patient perspectives.

name: Priya Nair
bio: Priya's research focuses on large language models for clinical text analysis, with a particular emphasis on low-resource languages and global mental health equity. She holds an MSc in Machine Learning from University College London.
